reasoning: The deadline for an official announcement is July 1, 2025, which is less than 8 weeks from the May 7, 2025 knowledge cutoff. On May 5, Premier Danielle Smith explicitly established that an independence referendum would only occur if a citizen-led petition succeeds. Under the newly introduced Bill 54, this process requires gathering approximately 177,000 signatures within a 120-day window. Even if organizers launch the petition immediately, completing the signature drive and having Elections Alberta verify them will take months, making it practically impossible to fulfill these requirements and officially schedule a referendum before July 1. Furthermore, bypassing this process to unilaterally schedule a vote would contradict Smith's stated strategy of managing separatist pressure via citizen initiative. Given the restrictive timeframe and logistical realities of the petition mechanism, an official scheduling announcement before July 1 is highly unlikely.
batch_queries: Before July 1, 2025, will the government of Alberta officially announce a date for an independence referendum?
